title: Brahma Chalisa
intro: Shri Brahma Chalisa - Hymn to Lord Brahma
description:
॥ Brahma Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Jai Brahma jai Swayambhu, chaturanana sukhamoola.
Karahu kripa nija dasa pai, rahahu sada anukoola.
Tum srijaka brahmanda ke, aja vidhi ghata nama.
Vishvavidhata kijeeyai, jana pai kripa lalama.

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jai jai kamalasan jagamoola, rahahu sada janapai anukoola.
Rupa chaturbhuja parama suhavana, tumhein ahain chaturdika aanana.
Raktavarna tava subhaga shareera, mastaka jatajuta gambheera.
Taake oopara mukuta birajai, dadhi shveta mahachhavi chhajai.
Shvetavastra dhare tuma sundara, hai yajnopaveeta ati manahara.
Kanana kundala subhaga birajahin, gala motina ki mala rajahin.
Charihu veda tumhin pragataye, divya jnana tribhuvanhin sikhaye.
Brahmaloka shubha dhama tumhara, akhila bhuvana mahan yasha bistara.
Ardhangini tava hai Savitri, apara nama hiye Gayatri.
Sarasvati tava suta manohara, veena vadini saba vidhi mundara.
Kamalasan para rahe biraje, tuma haribhakti saja saba saaje.
Ksheera sindhu sovata surabhupa, nabhi kamala bho pragata anupa.
Tehi para tuma aasina kripala, sada karahu santana pratipala.
Eka bara ki katha prachari, tuma kahana moha bhayeu mana bhari.
Kamalasan lakhi keenha bichara, aura na kou ahai sansara.
Taba tuma kamalanal gahi leenha, anta bilokana kara prana keenha.
Kotika varsha gaye yahi bhanti, bhramata bhramata beete dina rati.
Pai tuma taakara anta na paye, hvai nirasha atishaya du khiyaye.
Puni bichhara mana mahan yaha keenha, mahapagh yaha ati prachina.
Yaako janma bhayo ko karana, tabahin mohi karayo yaha dharana.
Akhila bhuvana mahan kahan koi nahin, saba kichhu ahai nihita mo mahin.
Yaha nishchaya kari garaba badhayo, nija kahana brahma mani sukhapaye.
Gagana gira taba bhayi gambheera, Brahma vachana sunahu dhari dheera.
Sakala srishti kara swami joi, brahma anadi alakha hai soi.
Nija ichha ina saba niramaye, Brahma Vishnu Mahesh banaye.
Srishti lagi pragate trayadeva, saba jaga inaki karihai seva.
Mahapagh jo tumharo aasana, ta pai ahai Vishnu ko shasana.
Vishnu nabhiten pragatyo aayi, tuma kahana satya deenha samujhai.
Bhautahu jaayi Vishnu hitamani, yaha kahi bandha bhayi nabhavani.
Taahi shravana kahi acharaja mana, puni chaturanana keenha payana.
Kamala nal dhari neeche aava, tahaan Vishnu ke darshana paava.
Shayana karata dekhe surabhupa, shayamavarna tanu parama anupa.
Sohata chaturbhuja atisundara, kreetamukata rajata mastaka para.
Gala baijanti mala birajai, koti surya ki shobha lajai.
Shankha chakra aru gada manohara, shesha naga shayya ati manahara.
Divyarupa lakhi keenha pranama, harshita bhe shreepati sukha dhamu.
Bahu vidhi vinaya keenha chaturanana, taba lakshmi pati kaheu mudita mana.
Brahma doori karahu abhimana, brahmarupa hama dau samana.
Teesare Shree Shivshankar aahin, brahmarupa saba tribhuvana manhin.
Tuma son hoi srishti vistara, hama palana karihain sansara.
Shiva samhara karihin saba kera, hama teenahun kahana kaja dhanera.
Agunarupa Shree Brahma bakhanahu, nirakara tinkahan tuma janahu.
Hama sakar rupa trayadeva, karihain sada brahma ki seva.
Yaha suni Brahma parama si haye, parabrahma ke yasha ati gaaye.
So saba vidita veda ke nama, mukti rupa so parama lalama.
Yahi vidhi prabhu bho janama tumhara, puni tuma pragata keenha sansara.
Nama pitamaha sundara payeu, jada chetana saba kahana niramayeu.
Leenha aneka bara avatara, sundara suyasha jagata vistara.
Devadanuja saba tuma kahana dhyavahin, manavanchhita tuma sana saba pavahin.
Jo kou dhyana dharai nara nari, taki aasa pujavahu sari.
Pushkar teertha parama sukhadai, tahaan tuma basahu sada surarai.
Kunda nahai karahi jo pujan, ta kara doora hoi saba dooshan.
meaning:
॥ Brahma Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Jai Brahma jai Swayambhu, chaturanana sukhamoola.
[Victory to Brahma, the self-manifested one, the four-faced root of happiness.]
Karhu kripa nija dasa pai, rahahu sada anukoola.
[Bestow your grace upon your devotee, and always remain favorable to me.]

Tum srijaka brahmanda ke, aja vidhi ghata nama.
[You are the creator of the universe, known by the names Aja (unborn) and Vidhi-Ghata.]
Vishvavidhata kijeeyai, jana pai kripa lalama.
[O creator of the world, bestow your beautiful grace upon your people.]

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jai jai kamalasan jagamoola, rahahu sada janapai anukoola.
[Victory to the lotus-seated root of the world, always remain favorable to your devotees.]
Rupa chaturbhuja parama suhavana, tumhein ahain chaturdika aanana.
[Your four-armed form is extremely beautiful, and you possess faces in all four directions.]
Raktavarna tava subhaga shareera, mastaka jatajuta gambheera.
[Your body is of red complexion, and your head has a grave cluster of matted hair.]
Taake oopara mukuta birajai, dadhi shveta mahachhavi chhajai.
[A crown rests upon it, and your white beard adds to your great splendor.]
Shvetavastra dhare tuma sundara, hai yajnopaveeta ati manahara.
[You wear beautiful white garments, and your sacred thread (Yajnopaveeta) is very captivating.]
Kanana kundala subhaga birajahin, gala motina ki mala rajahin.
[Beautiful earrings adorn your ears, and a necklace of pearls shines on your neck.]
Charihu veda tumhin pragataye, divya jnana tribhuvanhin sikhaye.
[You are the one who revealed the four Vedas and taught divine knowledge to the three worlds.]
Brahmaloka shubha dhama tumhara, akhila bhuvana mahan yasha bistara.
[Brahmaloka is your auspicious abode, and your glory is spread throughout the entire universe.]
Ardhangini tava hai Savitri, apara nama hiye Gayatri.
[Savitri is your better half, whose other name is Gayatri.]
Sarasvati tava suta manohara, veena vadini saba vidhi mundara.
[Sarasvati is your beautiful daughter, the player of the Veena, beautiful in every way.]
Kamalasan para rahe biraje, tuma haribhakti saja saba saaje.
[You remain seated on the lotus throne, adorned with the devotion to Hari (Vishnu).]
Ksheera sindhu sovata surabhupa, nabhi kamala bho pragata anupa.
[From the navel lotus of the sleeping Lord of Gods (Vishnu) in the ocean of milk, an incomparable lotus appeared.]
Tehi para tuma aasina kripala, sada karahu santana pratipala.
[O merciful one, you sit upon it and always protect the saints.]
Eka bara ki katha prachari, tuma kahana moha bhayeu mana bhari.
[There is a famous story that once, great delusion entered your mind.]
Kamalasan lakhi keenha bichara, aura na kou ahai sansara.
[Sitting on the lotus, you thought that there is no one else in this world besides me.]
Taba tuma kamalanal gahi leenha, anta bilokana kara prana keenha.
[Then you grabbed the stalk of the lotus and vowed to see its end.]
Kotika varsha gaye yahi bhanti, bhramata bhramata beete dina rati.
[Millions of years passed in this manner; days and nights spent wandering.]
Pai tuma taakara anta na paye, hvai nirasha atishaya du khiyaye.
[But you could not find the end and became extremely disappointed and sad.]
Puni bichhara mana mahan yaha keenha, mahapagh yaha ati prachina.
[Then you thought in your mind that this great lotus is very ancient.]
Yaako janma bhayo ko karana, tabahin mohi karayo yaha dharana.
[It must have a reason for birth, which is why it has held me.]
Akhila bhuvana mahan kahan koi nahin, saba kichhu ahai nihita mo mahin.
[There is no one else in the entire universe; everything is contained within me.]
Yaha nishchaya kari garaba badhayo, nija kahana brahma mani sukhapaye.
[Determining this, your pride increased, and you felt happy thinking you were the ultimate Brahma.]
Gagana gira taba bhayi gambheera, Brahma vachana sunahu dhari dheera.
[Then a deep voice came from the sky, "O Brahma, listen to my words with patience."]
Sakala srishti kara swami joi, brahma anadi alakha hai soi.
[The Lord of the entire creation is the one who is the beginningless and invisible Brahma.]
Nija ichha ina saba niramaye, Brahma Vishnu Mahesh banaye.
[By His own will, He created all these, and formed Brahma, Vishnu, and Mahesh.]
Srishti lagi pragate trayadeva, saba jaga inaki karihai seva.
[These three gods appeared for the creation, and the whole world will serve them.]
Mahapagh jo tumharo aasana, ta pai ahai Vishnu ko shasana.
[The great lotus that is your seat, is under the governance of Vishnu.]
Vishnu nabhiten pragatyo aayi, tuma kahana satya deenha samujhai.
[You have emerged from Vishnu's navel; this is the truth understood.]
Bhautahu jaayi Vishnu hitamani, yaha kahi bandha bhayi nabhavani.
[Go to Vishnu, considering it for your welfare; saying this, the celestial voice stopped.]
Taahi shravana kahi acharaja mana, puni chaturanana keenha payana.
[Hearing this, you felt amazed and then the four-faced one proceeded.]
Kamala nal dhari neeche aava, tahaan Vishnu ke darshana paava.
[Holding the lotus stalk, you came down and had the vision of Vishnu.]
Shayana karata dekhe surabhupa, shayamavarna tanu parama anupa.
[You saw the Lord of Gods sleeping, whose dark-complexioned body is incomparable.]
Sohata chaturbhuja atisundara, kreetamukata rajata mastaka para.
[He looked very beautiful with four arms, and a crown shone on his head.]
Gala baijanti mala birajai, koti surya ki shobha lajai.
[The Vaijayanti garland adorned his neck, putting the splendor of millions of suns to shame.]
Shankha chakra aru gada manohara, shesha naga shayya ati manahara.
[He held the conch, discus, and mace, and lay on the enchanting bed of Shesha Naga.]
Divyarupa lakhi keenha pranama, harshita bhe shreepati sukha dhamu.
[Seeing his divine form, you bowed, and the Lord of Lakshmi (Vishnu) became happy.]
Bahu vidhi vinaya keenha chaturanana, taba lakshmi pati kaheu mudita mana.
[The four-faced one prayed in many ways, then Lakshmi's husband said with a happy mind.]
Brahma doori karahu abhimana, brahmarupa hama dau samana.
[O Brahma, remove your pride; we both are equal in the form of Brahma.]
Teesare Shree Shivshankar aahin, brahmarupa saba tribhuvana manhin.
[The third is Lord Shivshankar; all of us are forms of Brahma in the three worlds.]
Tuma son hoi srishti vistara, hama palana karihain sansara.
[From you will come the expansion of creation, and we will protect the world.]
Shiva samhara karihin saba kera, hama teenahun kahana kaja dhanera.
[Shiva will destroy everything; all three of us have great work.]
Agunarupa Shree Brahma bakhanahu, nirakara tinkahan tuma janahu.
[Describe the formless Brahma, know Him to be without attributes (Nirakara).]
Hama sakar rupa trayadeva, karihain sada brahma ki seva.
[We three gods are in manifested forms and will always serve that Brahma.]
Yaha suni Brahma parama si haye, parabrahma ke yasha ati gaaye.
[Hearing this, Brahma became extremely pleased and sang the praises of the Supreme Brahma.]
So saba vidita veda ke nama, mukti rupa so parama lalama.
[All this is known by the names in the Vedas, the form of liberation and ultimate beauty.]
Yahi vidhi prabhu bho janama tumhara, puni tuma pragata keenha sansara.
[In this way, your birth happened, and then you manifested the world.]
Nama pitamaha sundara payeu, jada chetana saba kahana niramayeu.
[You received the beautiful name 'Pitamaha' and created all animate and inanimate things.]
Leenha aneka bara avatara, sundara suyasha jagata vistara.
[You took many incarnations and spread your beautiful glory in the world.]
Devadanuja saba tuma kahana dhyavahin, manavanchhita tuma sana saba pavahin.
[Gods and demons all meditate on you, and from you, everyone attains their desired boons.]
Jo kou dhyana dharai nara nari, taki aasa pujavahu sari.
[Whoever, man or woman, meditates on you, you fulfill all their hopes.]
Pushkar teertha parama sukhadai, tahaan tuma basahu sada surarai.
[Pushkar pilgrimage is most blissful; you always reside there, O King of Gods.]
Kunda nahai karahi jo pujan, ta kara doora hoi saba dooshan.
[Whoever bathes in the pond and worships, all their sins are removed.]

faq.q1: What is the main significance of the Brahma Chalisa?
faq.a1: Lord Brahma is the creator of the universe. Reciting this Chalisa is a way to seek blessings for wisdom, intellect, creativity, and new beginnings.
faq.q2: Does reading the Brahma Chalisa improve intellectual capacity?
faq.a2: Yes, Lord Brahma is the source of all knowledge and wisdom. Reciting it brings mental clarity and the intelligence needed to solve complex problems.
faq.q3: What is the best time to recite it?
faq.a3: 'Brahma Muhurta' (the time before sunrise) is considered the most suitable, as this time is believed to be the most powerful for creation and meditation.
faq.q4: Does it require any specific rituals?
faq.a4: No, Lord Brahma values simplicity. You can recite it with a pure mind and concentration. There is no need for any complex rituals.
faq.a5: Yes, anyone who desires the growth of knowledge, the birth of new ideas, and spiritual development can recite this Chalisa.
